summary: Added the dedicated Claude-only Omnigent host overlay, hermetic Compose contract coverage, and operator startup and validation guidance requested by the MM-1199 verifier.

tasks_file.notes: The prerequisite script could not resolve a MoonSpec feature packet on this Jira branch. No tasks.md was changed; the verifier remainingWork list was used as the authoritative bounded backlog.

gap_statuses.requirement: Provide a dedicated Claude-only Omnigent external host that consumes MoonMind's canonical Claude OAuth volume at /root/.claude.
gap_statuses.evidence: docker-compose.claude-host.yaml defines the omnigent-host-claude profile and hostname, HOME=/root, empty competing credential variables, writable claude_auth_volume:/root/.claude, isolated state, sanitized workspace mounts, required dependencies, local-network membership, and restart policy.

gap_statuses.requirement: Protect the dedicated Claude OAuth host contract with repo-local validation and operator guidance.
gap_statuses.evidence: tests/integration/temporal/test_compose_foundation.py covers the overlay contract and docs/Omnigent/CombinedStackValidationAndRollback.md documents startup and validation. Docker Compose rendering and a static Python contract check passed; pytest execution is unavailable because this runtime has no pytest module.

targeted_checks.notes: /usr/local/bin/python: No module named pytest. This matches the source verifier's environment note.

targeted_checks.command: docker compose -f docker-compose.yaml -f docker-compose.claude-host.yaml --profile omnigent-host-claude config --quiet
targeted_checks.notes: The merged base and Claude-host overlay configuration rendered successfully.
PyYAML loaded the overlay and verified HOME, all empty competing credentials, the canonical OAuth mount, and the claude-auth-init dependency.

environment_limitations: The focused pytest integration test could not execute because pytest is not installed in the managed runtime.
